name: TRIPOD-AI
description: Transparent Reporting of a multivariable prediction model for Individual Prognosis Or Diagnosis — AI extension.

items.title: Title
items.description: Identify the study as developing or evaluating the performance of a multivariable prediction model using machine learning or AI, the target population, and the outcome to be predicted.

items.title: Abstract
items.description: Provide a structured summary of objectives, study design, setting, participants, data sources, predictors, outcomes, statistical analysis, results, and conclusions.

items.title: Background and rationale
items.description: Explain the medical context (including whether diagnostic or prognostic) and rationale for developing or evaluating the prediction model, including references to existing models.

items.title: Objectives
items.description: Specify the objectives, including whether the study describes the development or validation of the model, or both.

items.title: Source of data
items.description: Describe the study design or source of data (e.g. randomised trial, cohort, EHR), and key dates including start and end of data collection.

items.title: Participants
items.description: Specify key elements of the study setting (e.g. primary care, secondary care, general population), including number and location of centres; describe eligibility criteria for participants.

items.title: Outcome
items.description: Clearly define the outcome that is predicted by the model, including how and when assessed; specify any actions to blind assessment of the outcome to be predicted.

items.title: Predictors
items.description: Clearly define all predictors used in developing or validating the model, including how and when measured; specify any actions to blind assessment of predictors for the outcome and other predictors.

items.title: Sample size
items.description: Explain how the study size was arrived at.

items.title: Missing data
items.description: Describe how missing data were handled (e.g. complete-case analysis, single imputation, multiple imputation) with details of any imputation method.

items.title: Statistical analysis: development
items.description: Describe how predictors were handled in the analyses; specify the type of model, all model-building procedures (including any predictor selection), and method for internal validation.

items.title: Statistical analysis: validation
items.description: For validation, describe how the predictions were calculated; describe any re-calibration of the model or its predictors.

items.title: Risk groups
items.description: Provide details on how risk groups were created, if done.

items.title: Development vs. validation
items.description: For validation, identify any differences from the development data in setting, eligibility criteria, outcome, and predictors.

items.title: AI/ML methods
items.description: Describe the type of AI/ML algorithm used, the rationale for choosing it, the hyperparameters and how they were tuned, the software, version, and computational resources used.

items.title: Fairness and bias
items.description: Describe any pre-specified fairness or bias analyses across protected groups and how potential algorithmic bias was assessed and mitigated.

items.title: Explainability and interpretability
items.description: Describe any analyses for model explainability or interpretability (e.g. SHAP, LIME, partial dependence plots).

items.title: Participants flow
items.description: Describe the flow of participants through the study, including the number with and without the outcome and (if applicable) a summary of follow-up time; consider a flow diagram.

items.title: Participants characteristics
items.description: Describe the characteristics of the participants (basic demographics, clinical features, available predictors), including the number with missing data for predictors and outcome.

items.title: Model development
items.description: Specify the number of participants and outcome events in each analysis; for any unadjusted association between each candidate predictor and outcome, provide the corresponding estimate.

items.title: Model specification
items.description: Present the full prediction model to allow predictions for individuals (e.g. formula, code, or web link); explain how to use the prediction model.

items.title: Model performance
items.description: Report performance measures (with CIs) for the prediction model and any comparators.

items.title: Model updating
items.description: If applicable, report the results of any model updating (e.g. recalibration) resulting from the validation, either overall or in pre-specified subgroups.

items.title: Limitations
items.description: Discuss any limitations of the study (such as non-representative sample, few events per predictor, missing data, including potential bias from algorithmic decisions or training data).

items.title: Interpretation
items.description: Give an overall interpretation of the results, considering objectives, limitations, results from similar studies, and other relevant evidence; discuss clinical utility.

items.title: Implications
items.description: Discuss the potential clinical use of the model and implications for future research, including any planned external validation or impact studies.

items.title: Supplementary information, funding, and code availability
items.description: Provide information about the availability of supplementary resources (study protocol, web calculator, datasets, training code, trained model); state the source of funding and conflicts of interest.
